author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
Who May Be Said to Walk Without Spot; Damnable and Venial Sins.
title: Who May Be Said to Walk Without Spot; Damnable and Venial Sins.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Chapter IX.—(20.)

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Who May Be Said to Walk Without Spot; Damnable and Venial Sins.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Having premised these remarks, let us carefully attend to the passages which he whom we are answering has produced, as if we ourselves had quoted them.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “In Deuteronomy, ‘Thou shalt be perfect before the Lord thy God.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Again, in the same book, ‘There shall be not an imperfect man among the sons of Israel.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: In like manner the Saviour says in the Gospel, Be ye perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So the apostle, in his second Epistle to the Corinthians, says: ‘Finally, brethren, farewell.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Be perfect.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Again, to the Colossians he writes: ‘Warning every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And so to the Philippians: ‘Do all things without murmurings and disputings, that ye may be blameless, and harmless, as the immaculate sons of God.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: In like manner to the Ephesians he writes: ‘Blessed be the God and father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ; according as He hath chosen us in Him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and blameless before Him.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Then again to the Colossians he says in another passage: ‘And you, that were sometime alienated, and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now hath He reconciled in the body of His flesh through death; present yourselves holy and unblameable and unreprovable in His sight.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: In the same strain, he says to the Ephesians: ‘That He might present to Himself a glorious Church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing but that it should be holy and without blemish.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So in his first Epistle to the Corinthians he says ‘Be ye sober, and righteous, and sin not.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So again in the Epistle of St. Peter it is written: ‘Wherefore gird up the loins of your mind, be sober, and hope to the end, for the grace that is offered to you: . . . as obedient children, not fashioning yourselves according to the former lusts in your ignorance: but as He who hath called you is holy, so be ye holy in all manner of conversation; because it is written, Be ye holy; for I am holy.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Whence blessed David likewise says: ‘O Lord, who shall sojourn in Thy tabernacle, or who shall rest on Thy holy mountain?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He that walketh without blame, and worketh righteousness.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And in another passage: ‘I shall be blameless with Him.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And yet again: ‘Blessed are the blameless in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: To the same effect it is written in Solomon: ‘The Lord loveth holy hearts, and all they that are blameless are acceptable unto Him.’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Now some of these passages exhort men who are running their course that they run perfectly; others refer to the end thereof, that men may reach forward to it as they run.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He, however, is not unreasonably said to walk blamelessly, not who has already reached the end of his journey, but who is pressing on towards the end in a blameless manner, free from damnable sins, and at the same time not neglecting to cleanse by almsgiving such sins as are venial.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For the way in which we walk, that is, the road by which we reach perfection, is cleansed by clean prayer.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: That, however, is a clean prayer in which we say in truth, “Forgive us, as we ourselves forgive.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So that, as there is nothing censured when blame is not imputed, we may hold on our course to perfection without censure, in a word, blamelessly; and in this perfect state, when we arrive at it at last, we shall find that there is absolutely nothing which requires cleansing by forgiveness.
